criteria.description: GET /health returns HTTP 200 with JSON service status.

criteria.description: POST /capture/water accepts raw JPEG bytes and returns persisted metadata on success.

criteria.description: Capture endpoint rejects missing/invalid API keys and never returns or logs token values.

criteria.description: Configured device allowlist rejects unknown X-Device-Id values.

criteria.description: Invalid methods, paths, lengths, media types, sizes, and non-JPEG bodies return deterministic JSON errors.

criteria.description: Successful captures are written under <root>/captures/water/<YYYY-MM>/ using the configured timezone.

criteria.description: Image bytes and latest metadata are written atomically; latest.json is updated only after image persistence succeeds.

criteria.description: Multiple uploads in one month are preserved; latest.json points to the newest successful upload.

criteria.description: Month keys are computed in the configured timezone, defaulting to Europe/Warsaw.

criteria.description: Host, port, root, API key, timezone, size limits, and allowed devices are loaded from environment variables.

criteria.description: Every success and error response is JSON with stable fields.
